meta_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity project template to Solve no design skills (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no design skills. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelancer Guide: Use Antigravity Templates to Solve Design Gaps
content.seo_description: Overcome design limitations as a freelancer. Learn how to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to ship professional SaaS apps at record speed.
content.answer_block: Stop letting design gaps stall your freelance projects. By leveraging the Antigravity project template with Claude Code, you can scaffold production-ready, aesthetically refined UIs in minutes. This workflow automates the heavy lifting, allowing you to focus on core business logic while ensuring your client output maintains a premium, high-converting design standard.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank IDE, the client deadline is breathing down your neck, and you realize your design skills are nowhere near the level of the premium product you promised. It’s the classic freelancer’s bottleneck: you have the logic, but the UI looks like a relic from 2005. The pressure to deliver high-end visuals while maintaining development velocity is a recipe for burnout.

But what if you didn't have to design from scratch? The Antigravity project template acts as your architectural foundation, providing pre-refined UI patterns that look like they were touched by a senior design agency. Combined with Claude Code, you aren't just coding—you're orchestrating a professional-grade build that bridges the gap between 'developer code' and 'client-ready product'.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Foundation
steps.description: Start by pulling the core template to ensure you have a clean, modular architecture that supports rapid UI iteration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity standard SaaS template, including Tailwind CSS integration and component-based structure.

steps.title: Define UI Constraints
steps.description: Establish your design language early to prevent visual drift. This keeps the aesthetic consistent across all modules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up a global theme config with a modern color palette, typography scaling, and spacing constants based on professional SaaS standards.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "update tailwind.config.js with a primary brand color of #4F46E5 and a modern sans-serif stack"

steps.title: Component Scaffolding
steps.description: Generate the heavy UI components using the template’s existing component library to maintain visual harmony.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a responsive dashboard layout with sidebar navigation, header, and data-grid views using pre-defined Antigravity components.

steps.title: Logic Integration
steps.description: Inject your business logic into the scaffolded UI without breaking the layout integrity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate API service layer for authentication and data fetching, mapping the results to the previously generated UI components.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "connect the dashboard data grid to the mock API service and handle loading/error states"

steps.title: Polishing & Responsive Refinement
steps.description: Review the build for mobile responsiveness and fine-tune spacing to ensure a premium finish.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the current layout for mobile responsiveness and apply minor spacing adjustments to match the Antigravity design system guidelines.

pro_tips: Always lock your Tailwind configuration file early; changing global spacing scales after 50+ components will lead to massive refactoring debt.
Use Claude Code's '--verbose' flag during complex UI generation to debug how the template is resolving component dependencies.
Create a 'Custom Components' directory outside the Antigravity core to avoid overwriting your unique logic during template updates.
Implement 'Visual Regression' testing early; even simple snapshots can save hours of manual cross-browser debugging.
Leverage the Antigravity 'Hooks' library to handle state management, keeping your view components lean and focused purely on rendering.
content.next_steps: Once your first project is live, audit your component library, create your own custom 'Antigravity-lite' template for future freelance projects, and begin automating your deployment pipeline.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting with CSS and start shipping products your clients love. With the Antigravity workflow, you’ve got the speed of a senior engineer and the aesthetic of a pro agency. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
